locked_facts: Concave lens has negative focal length
Always produces virtual, erect, diminished image
Image is ALWAYS between F and optical centre on same side as object
Used in spectacles for myopia (short-sightedness) correction
Power P = 1/f, negative for concave lens
truth_statements: A concave lens is thinner at the centre and diverges light rays
A concave lens always forms a virtual, erect and diminished image
The image formed by a concave lens is always between the focus and the optical centre
Concave lenses are used to correct myopia
The focal length of a concave lens is negative

aha_moment.what_to_show: Move the object from far away to very close to the lens. Despite the huge range of object distances, the image is ALWAYS virtual, erect, and diminished — it stays trapped between F and the optical centre. No matter what you do, a concave lens NEVER forms a real image. This is the key difference from a convex lens.
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Show the virtual image staying within the F-to-O region regardless of object position. Highlight that the image size changes but never exceeds the object size.

simulation_states.label: Object far from lens — virtual image near F
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Object far left. Rays diverge after lens. Virtual image (dotted arrow) close to F, very small.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: Even though the object is far away, the concave lens forms a tiny virtual image near F.

simulation_states.label: Object closer — image moves toward O
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Object moved closer. Virtual image is now between F and O, slightly larger but still diminished.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: As object approaches, image moves from F toward O, growing slightly but always smaller than object.

simulation_states.label: Key insight — image always virtual, erect, diminished
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Multiple object positions shown. All images are on the same side, erect, and smaller.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: Unlike a convex lens, a concave lens ALWAYS forms the same type of image. No position change can make it form a real or magnified image.

simulation_states.label: Interactive — move the object
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Slider to change object distance. Virtual image updates in real time.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: Verify: no matter where you place the object, the image is always virtual, erect, and diminished.

trigger_keywords: concave lens se real banta hai
negative lens real image de sakta hai
common_misconceptions.student_belief: A concave lens can form a real image in some special cases
common_misconceptions.actual_physics: A concave lens ALWAYS forms a virtual, erect, and diminished image regardless of object position. It diverges light rays — they never converge to form a real image.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show ray diagrams at multiple object positions — the refracted rays always diverge. Only their backward extensions (dotted) meet, forming a virtual image.

legend.meaning: Image distance (always negative = same side as object)
